dataset: Synthetic fuel cell voltage stability dataset

pi_items.source: Task description (item 6) and export_result.sh line 5 specify TARGET_FILE=/home/ga/Desktop/Voltage_Stability_Report.pbix
Task description items 2-4 explicitly require these three DAX measures: Global_Mean_Voltage (average of entire dataset), UCL (Mean + 3*Sigma), LCL (Mean - 3*Sigma)
Task description item 5: 'Create a Line Chart on a page named Stability Analysis plotting Timestamp on X-axis and Stack_Voltage, Global_Mean_Voltage, UCL, and LCL on Y-axis'

These are the standard DAX functions for implementing global context measures (ignoring row filters). ALL() and REMOVEFILTERS() remove filter context; CALCULATE() modifies context. Verified in verifier.py lines 73-76

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data generated by Python script
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 13-44: Python script generates synthetic fuel cell data using random.gauss() for noise and deterministic drift
setup_task.sh line 28: for i in range(3000) generates 3000 data points at 1 Hz sampling rate
setup_task.sh line 19: start_time = datetime.datetime(2023, 10, 1, 8, 0, 0); 3000 seconds of 1 Hz data = 50 minutes duration
setup_task.sh line 33: drift = -0.000005 * i (simulates very slow fuel cell degradation)
Calculated from 3000 samples with base=0.72 and N(0,0.015) noise plus drift. Actual value varies due to randomness but mean converges to ~0.7123
Sample standard deviation of voltage data from random.gauss(0, 0.015) noise component with additional drift variation

privileged_info_summary: This task involves a synthetic fuel cell voltage stability dataset with 3000 samples collected at 1 Hz on 2023-10-01 from 08:00:00 to 08:49:59. The data has four columns: Timestamp, Stack_Voltage (mean ~0.712V with Gaussian noise N(0,0.015) and slow drift -0.000005V/s), Current_Density (constant 1.5 A/cm²), and Inlet_Temp (N(65.0, 0.5) Celsius). The task requires creating a Power BI report named 'Voltage_Stability_Report.pbix' with DAX measures for Global_Mean_Voltage, UCL (Mean+3*Sigma), and LCL (Mean-3*Sigma), displayed as a line chart. All metadata claims are verified correct.
